title: SRTM Elevation Bands
description: SRTM digital elevation model classified into 100 m elevation bands (0–5000 m). Demonstrates the Invertible conformance level: any raw elevation value maps deterministically to its enclosing band via /inverse.

elevation.description: Elevation bands (metres above sea level). 100 m step from 0 to 5000 m produces 51 bands.

sort_by.description: Output field to sort members by. 'code' sorts by numeric lower-bound string (default, ascending = lowest band first). 'index' sorts by sequential band index (same ordering as 'code' for ascending).

sort_dir.description: Sort direction. 'desc' returns bands from highest to lowest elevation.

code.description: String representation of the band's lower bound (e.g. '100' for the 100–199 m band). Used as the member identifier.

index.description: Zero-based sequential index of this band within the extent (0 for the 0–99 m band, 1 for the 100–199 m band, etc.).

lower.description: Inclusive lower bound of the band in metres (equals lower bound of the containing step interval).

upper.description: Inclusive upper bound of the band in metres (lower + step - 1). For the last band this equals the dimension extent maximum.

elevation.description: Digital elevation model value in metres.
